pathophysiology_summary: Hyperadrenergic POTS involves excessive sympathetic activation on standing, producing a marked postural tachycardia with a hypertensive/adrenergic surge, tremor, anxiety and post-exertional fatigue that settle on lying flat. This mimics SVT/AF, thyroid storm and phaeochromocytoma. Reassuring features are strictly postural symptoms that settle supine, no rest palpitations, no spontaneous (non-postural) surges, and no thyrotoxic features. However, telehealth cannot obtain an ECG or auscultate, so an arrhythmia (or, less likely, an endocrine cause) cannot be excluded remotely, and the presyncope creates a fall risk. The correct action is supportive counter-manoeuvres, salt/fluids and rest plus urgent in-person review with an ECG — not home benzodiazepines/sympatholytics — with 000 for rest tachycardia persisting supine, crushing chest pain or true syncope.

differential.evidence_basis: Postural adrenergic surge on a known background.
Tachycardia demands ECG to exclude arrhythmia.
Surges/hypertension; but strictly postural argues against.
Postural-only, settles supine, no rest palpitations/spontaneous surges/thyroid features.
Lower probability but not excludable without ECG.
No thyrotoxic features; TFTs on follow-up.
Working telehealth diagnosis: hyperadrenergic POTS surge; confirm with in-person ECG/postural vitals, support with counter-manoeuvres/salt-fluids, and manage fall risk.

red_flags.description: Tachycardia persisting >130 bpm while lying flat, or palpitations at rest
red_flags.clinical_significance: Rest tachycardia suggests a primary arrhythmia needing urgent ECG.

red_flags.description: Crushing chest pain or true syncope
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would indicate cardiac ischaemia or a serious arrhythmia.
Would raise phaeochromocytoma/thyrotoxicosis needing endocrine work-up.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Diagnosing POTS without an ECG to exclude arrhythmia.
Starting benzodiazepines/clonidine/beta-blockers over chat before diagnosis.
Not addressing presyncope/fall risk in an isolated patient.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Postural HR rise >30 bpm with hypertensive surge (hyperadrenergic pattern)
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports POTS with ECG confirmation and conservative management.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Reliable postural vitals and cardiac auscultation
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Screen arrhythmia/endocrine red flags, provide counter-manoeuvres/salt-fluids and fall-safety, arrange urgent in-person review with an ECG, and escalate to 000 for rest tachycardia, chest pain or true syncope.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Starting sedatives/sympatholytics remotely or reassuring without an ECG could miss an arrhythmia/endocrine cause.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Heart Rhythm Society expert consensus on POTS
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: POTS is defined by a sustained postural HR rise >30 bpm; management begins with non-drug measures, with medications specialist-guided after an ECG.
Palpitations/tachycardia warrant ECG to exclude arrhythmic/structural causes before attribution to a benign cause.
